title: Fix semantic search deadlines

tasks.title: Add regression tests for semantic deadline behavior
tasks.verification: RED evidence: `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest tests/test_storage.py::TestSearch::test_search_hybrid_does_not_create_indexes_on_query_path tests/test_tool_routing.py -q` failed with the new hot-path guard and fallback-guidance tests while existing surrounding routing tests passed.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added storage test asserting live hybrid search does not call `create_fts_index`/`create_index` and falls back to vector results. Added tool-routing test asserting packaged instructions mention timeout/deadline retry with `hybrid:false`. RED run failed as expected before implementation.
tasks.notes: Added failing regression tests for hybrid search hot-path index creation and timeout fallback guidance. Checkpoint commit 32e335f.

error_recovery.last_error: Initial `python -m pytest` lacked pytest; `uv run pytest` without dev extra collected with system pytest and missed lancedb. Switched to `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest`.
error_recovery.next_strategy: Use `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest` for project tests.

attempts.error: No module named pytest / ModuleNotFoundError: lancedb under wrong pytest invocation
attempts.diagnosis: Test environment needed uv dev extra and python -m pytest to use project venv.
attempts.fix_tried: Reran with `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest`.

tasks.title: Refactor hybrid index readiness and fallback
tasks.verification: GREEN evidence: `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest tests/test_storage.py -q` passed: 33 passed in 1.73s. Focused hot-path tests also passed.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added best-effort LanceDB index readiness probing on table open. Added `prepare_hybrid_indexes()` for explicit indexing paths and invoked it after `Indexer.index_all()`. Removed `replace=True` hot-path index creation from `search_hybrid`; when FTS is not ready, search degrades to `search_vector()` with log context. Updated storage tests to reflect explicit preparation.
tasks.notes: Refactored hybrid search to avoid live query index creation and fall back to vector when FTS is not ready. Checkpoint commit 840eaea.

tasks.title: Update setup and agent guidance for Vision/OpenCode lgrep usage
tasks.verification: GREEN evidence: `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest tests/test_tool_routing.py -q` passed: 43 passed, 2 skipped in 0.04s.
tasks.implementation_summary: Documented Vision/OpenCode tuning in README and skill docs: worktree dedup, explicit warm paths, no auto-warm-all-disk, and lgrep timeout below proxy deadline. Updated always-on instruction and skill usage to retry once with `hybrid:false` after hybrid semantic timeout/deadline, then fall back to symbols/text/read.
tasks.notes: Updated Vision/OpenCode setup guidance and agent timeout fallback. Checkpoint commit 609f3f5.

tasks.title: Verify semantic search behavior end-to-end
tasks.verification: Verification evidence: focused suite `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest tests/test_storage.py tests/test_tool_routing.py -q` passed 76 passed, 2 skipped; full suite `uv run --extra dev python -m pytest -q` passed 575 passed, 2 skipped; `uv run --extra dev ruff check .` passed; worktree CLI probe against `/home/jon/dev/advance` returned vector results without timeout.
tasks.implementation_summary: Ran focused and full test suites, lint, and a worktree lgrep CLI probe against `/home/jon/dev/advance`. No additional code changes were needed for verification.
tasks.notes: Completed end-to-end verification. Worktree clean at 609f3f5.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved execution with 'execute all'; proposal captures the lgrep semantic timeout problem and success criteria.

discovery.approval_evidence: Agreement artifact captures objectives, AC, constraints, and avoidances; typed contract minted.

design.approval_evidence: Design validator returned PASS WITH CONCERNS; required refinements were incorporated: index probing, index_all/warmup creation, and server-side fallback primary.

planning.approval_evidence: Task graph created with TDD intent, dependencies, and contract refs. User already approved execution with 'execute all'.

execution.approval_evidence: All planned tasks completed with checkpoints. Verification: full pytest 575 passed, 2 skipped; ruff passed; worktree CLI probe returned vector results without timeout.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User approved acceptance with 'approve'. Contract review matrix has 13 rows, 0 failing; independent reviewer verdict READY / PASS; tests and probes passed.

items.text: `lgrep_search_semantic` on a populated cache with >10k chunks does not create/replace FTS/vector indexes on the live query hot path.
If hybrid search cannot use ready indexes, lgrep returns useful vector-only results or a structured, actionable error within the configured lgrep timeout.
The configured lgrep server timeout is lower than the proxy/tool deadline so agents see lgrep-owned errors instead of `context deadline exceeded` where possible.
Local setup docs or checked-in examples include `LGREP_WORKTREE_DEDUP`, explicit warm paths, and timeout guidance for Vision/OpenCode usage.
Tool-selection guidance says: after one hybrid semantic timeout/deadline, retry once with `hybrid:false` and a small limit, then fall back to symbol/text/read.
Tests cover the hybrid hot-path guard/fallback and timeout guidance.
Preserve existing semantic and symbol MCP tool names.
Preserve stdio/local defaults for general users.
Do not add symlink/env hacks as substitutes for code-level latency control.
Keep behavior structural: readiness state, config, tests; no prose-only fix.
Do not mask all search failures as generic timeouts.
Do not require users/agents to manually run indexing before first semantic search.
Do not make every cached repo warm automatically in this local setup.

rows.evidence: tests/test_storage.py::TestSearch::test_search_hybrid_does_not_create_indexes_on_query_path passed; reviewer confirmed `search_hybrid` no longer creates indexes on live query path.
Vector fallback path exercised by storage test and worktree CLI probe against `/home/jon/dev/advance` returned vector results without timeout.
rows.notes: Structured timeout was also verified after Vision config with LGREP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_S=8.

rows.evidence: `~/.config/vision/servers.yaml` was updated, validated, reloaded, and lgrep restarted with `LGREP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_S=8`; hybrid probe returned lgrep-owned structured timeout instead of Vision context deadline.
rows.notes: Repo docs describe setting timeout below proxy/client deadline.

rows.evidence: README.md and skills/lgrep/SKILL.md include `LGREP_WORKTREE_DEDUP`, explicit `LGREP_WARM_PATHS`, `LGREP_AUTO_WARM_DISK=false`, and `LGREP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_S` guidance.
rows.notes: Local Vision config was applied first.
Guidance present in instructions, README, and skill docs.

rows.evidence: Focused suite passed 76 passed, 2 skipped; full suite passed 575 passed, 2 skipped; ruff passed.
rows.notes: Regression tests cover hot-path guard and timeout guidance.

rows.evidence: No MCP tool names or public tool registrations changed; changes are storage/indexing/docs/tests only.
Docs preserve stdio/local defaults and present Vision tuning as agent-heavy setup guidance.
No symlink/wrapper workaround introduced; code-level fallback and index-preparation changes were implemented.
Behavior encoded in `ChunkStore.prepare_hybrid_indexes`, `search_hybrid` fallback, and regression tests.
Existing structured `ToolError` timeout remains; search failures are not globally masked. Hybrid missing-index case degrades specifically to vector results.
First semantic search still auto-loads/auto-indexes; users are not required to manually index before searching.
Vision/OpenCode docs and applied config use explicit warm paths plus `LGREP_AUTO_WARM_DISK=false`, not warm-all-caches.

acceptanceCriteria: `lgrep_search_semantic` on a populated cache with >10k chunks does not create/replace FTS/vector indexes on the live query hot path.
If hybrid search cannot use ready indexes, lgrep returns useful vector-only results or a structured, actionable error within the configured lgrep timeout.
The configured lgrep server timeout is lower than the proxy/tool deadline so agents see lgrep-owned errors instead of `context deadline exceeded` where possible.
Local setup docs or checked-in examples include `LGREP_WORKTREE_DEDUP`, explicit warm paths, and timeout guidance for Vision/OpenCode usage.
Tool-selection guidance says: after one hybrid semantic timeout/deadline, retry once with `hybrid:false` and a small limit, then fall back to symbol/text/read.
Tests cover the hybrid hot-path guard/fallback and timeout guidance.
